details: In order to improve the process in curbing the high rate of mortality and infant mortality rate, the Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Odisha introduced a scheme named "
" - a conditional cash transfer maternity benefit scheme in 2011.
Contribute in reducing the maternal and infant mortality.
Improve the health and nutrition status of pregnant and lactating mother and their infants.
To provide partial wage loss compensation for the pregnant and nursing mothers with the aim to provide complete rest before and after the child birth.
Increase in utilization of child health services especially immunization.
Improve mother and child care practices.
benefits: Rs.3,000 shall be given during the third trimester of the pregnancy of the mother till delivery on completing the following 5 conditions:
Pregnancy registered at AWC/Mini AWC
Received at least 2 antenatal checkup (ANC)
Received IFA and calcium tablets.
Received at least 1 TT vaccination (out of optimal 2)
Received at least 1 counselling session at AWC/Village Health/ Home Visit.
The first 4 conditions shall be verified from MCP Card. On completing the above requirements first instalment shall be released.
Rs.2,000 shall be after completion of 10 months after delivery on fulfilment of following conditions:
Child birth is registered at AWC or HSC.
Child has received BCG vaccination.
Child has received Pentavalent 1 & 2 & 3 vaccinations.
Child has received Polio 1 & 2 & 3 vaccinations.
Child has received Rotavirus 1 & 2 & 3 vaccinations.
Child has received Measles vaccine before attaining 1 year of age.
Child has received Vitamin-A first dose before attaining 1 year of age
Mother has attended at least six Growth Monitoring and IYCF counselling sessions after delivery till 9 months
Child has been exclusively breastfed for first six months (self-certification by the beneficiary)
Child has been introduced to complementary food on completion of six months of age (self- certification by the beneficiary)
Child is being fed age-appropriate complementary feeding and continuing (self- certification by the beneficiary)
Note:
Overall, Rs.5,000 in two instalments as incentive is provided to mothers.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Odisha.
The scheme is primarily aimed at pregnant women.
Pregnant & Lactating women of 19 years of age and above for the first two live births, except all Government/Public Sector Undertakings (Central and State) employees and their wives will be covered.

offline: MAMATA mobile Application may be downloaded using URL: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.emamata.odisha&hl=en_IN&pli=1
The beneficiary may also log in on the department portal to apply for the scheme. URL: https://emamata.odisha.gov.in/login
In case the beneficiary has forgotten the password, the same can be reset by visiting URL : https://emamata.odisha.gov.in/public/forgot/password

documents_required: Proof of Identity (any 1 of the following):
Proof of Residence (any 1 of the following):
Rent Agreement or Property Tax Receipt
Medical Certificate or Pregnancy Record from a government health center or Anganwadi center
Certificate issued by a government authority proving your family's income (if income criteria apply)
Bank passbook or a recent bank statement for the account where the financial assistance will be credited
Aadhaar Enrollment Slip or Acknowledgement (if Aadhaar card is not yet received)
Recent passport-sized photographs of the applicant
Maternity registration record or any relevant documents from health services.
Any documentation if you have received benefits from similar schemes previously.

faqs.question: Is this scheme a Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) Scheme?
Are applications for this scheme open throughout the year?
Where the beneficiaries will register?
faqs.answer: Pregnancy registered at the AWC/Mini AWC.

faqs.question: How many antenatal check-up(ANC)?
faqs.answer: Received at least 2 by ANM by using MCP Card and Mother andChild Tracking System(MCTS)/Reproductive and Child Helath System(RCHS).

faqs.question: How to Receive IFA tablets?
faqs.answer: Through ANM by using MCP Card and MCTS/RCHS.

faqs.question: how to get counseling session?
faqs.answer: Receive at least one counseling session at the AWC/Village Health and Nutrition Day (VHND)/Home Visit by Scheme and ICDS Register.

faqs.question: How to get Child BCG vaccination?
faqs.answer: By ANM through MCP Card, MCTS/RCHS.

faqs.question: How to get Child Polio-1 & 2 & 3 vaccination?
faqs.answer: By ANM through MCP Card, MCTS/RCHS.

faqs.question: How to get Child Rotavirus 1 & 2 & 3 vaccinations?
faqs.answer: By ANM through MCP Card, MCTS/RCHS.

faqs.question: How to receive Child Mealses vaccine before attaining 1 year of age?
faqs.answer: By ANM through MCP Card, MCTS/RCHS.

faqs.question: How to get more details about Mamata Scheme?
faqs.answer: To get more details, Please visit https://wcd.odisha.gov.in/ICDS/mamata
